name: Bittersweet Chocolate and Lemon Tartlets
servings: makes six (4 to 4½-inch / 10 to 11-cm) tartlets or one (9 to 9½-inch / 23 to 24-cm) tart
intro: Almonds and dark chocolate are a favorite combination of mine and are the foundation of these tartlets. The crunchy Almond Cookie Crust is glazed with lemon olive oil ganache, which is made from the Lemon Olive Oil Truffle recipe. (I told you ganache is versatile!) The rich yet light lemon filling is nestled between bottom and top coatings of ganache.

ingredients.item: recipe Lemon Olive Oil Truffles (page 44: make the ganache but do not form truffles)
recipe Almond Cookie Crust (page 155), blind baked and cooled
plus ⅓ cup / 220 grams canned unsweetened full-fat coconut milk, well-stirred (do not use light)
/ 4.5 grams finely grated lemon zest, divided
/ 57 grams whole almonds, chopped, for garnish (substitute plump berries)

method: Measure the agar into a medium saucepan. Pour ½ cup / 120 ml of water over the agar, but do not stir or heat. Set aside for 15 minutes or longer to allow the agar to soften. This will help the agar dissolve thoroughly and easily.
Warm the ganache, if necessary, until liquefied in a water bath. Spread the crusts with a thin layer of ganache. Refrigerate until the ganache is set.

Bring the agar-water to a boil over medium heat, stirring a few times. Reduce the heat to low. Cover the saucepan and simmer for 5 to 6 minutes, stirring a few times to release any bits of agar that may be stuck on the bottom of the pan. Check a spoonful of liquid for specks of agar. Cover and simmer longer, if necessary, until the agar is completely dissolved.
Add the coconut milk, sugar, half the zest, salt, and turmeric if using. Cover the pan and simmer for 10 minutes.

In a small bowl, dissolve the arrowroot in ¼ cup / 60 ml of water. Whisking continuously, add the dissolved arrowroot to the saucepan. Cook just until the mixture boils and immediately remove from the heat. The mixture will be thick.

Stir the remaining zest, lemon juice, and vanilla into the cream. Pour into a shallow bowl, stirring a few times until the cream is not hot but still warm. The cream sets up fast. Pour into the ganache-glazed tart crust.

After about 10 minutes, the filling should be soft but not set. Pour enough of the ganache over the cream to cover each tartlet (1½ to 2 tablespoons / 22.5 to 30 ml), but let a bit of the bright yellow cream peek through. (Alternatively, you can drizzle the ganache decoratively.)
